meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn to implement SaaS pricing and overcome version control issues. A professional intermediate-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To solve version control issues while implementing SaaS pricing, leverage Claude Code to automate git workflows alongside Antigravity to structure your billing logic. By decoupling pricing schemas from your core codebase and utilizing automated commit tagging, you ensure that billing changes are audit-trailed, immutable, and easily revertible for your production environment.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect feature set, only to realize that every minor tweak to your pricing logic is overwriting your production environment. The fear of 'deploying and breaking' without a safety net isn't just a technical hurdle—it's a career-stalling bottleneck that keeps your best work hidden behind a fear of failure.

It’s time to stop coding in a vacuum. By integrating robust version control with a modular SaaS pricing engine, you transform your project from a fragile script into a professional-grade SaaS product. This guide bridges the gap between 'student project' and 'market-ready software' using the industry's most powerful AI-assisted development tools.

steps.title: Establish Versioned Environments
steps.description: Initialize a repository structure that separates your core logic from pricing configurations, ensuring you never push breaking changes to production again.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a directory structure for a SaaS app that strictly separates 'billing-logic' from 'core-features' and initialize a git-friendly configuration schema.

steps.title: Architect the Pricing Schema
steps.description: Define your pricing tiers as JSON-based configuration files to allow for versioning outside of the main binary code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a scalable pricing schema using a JSON configuration that supports monthly/annual cycles and feature flags.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create pricing_config.json with tiers for Free, Pro, and Enterprise'

steps.title: Automate Git Commit Hygiene
steps.description: Use Claude Code to enforce commit standards, ensuring every pricing change is documented with a specific intent and linked to a feature flag.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a pre-commit hook script that validates the structure of pricing_config.json before allowing a commit.

steps.title: Implement Feature Flagging
steps.description: Decouple your pricing logic from the UI to ensure you can toggle features without modifying the core codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current codebase to implement feature flags based on the user's active subscription tier from the pricing schema.

steps.title: Deploy with Rollback Safety
steps.description: Verify your deployment pipeline by simulating a pricing update and ensuring the system supports instant reverts via Git tags.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a deployment script that tags the current commit with the pricing version and prepares a rollback command.

pro_tips: Always store your pricing configuration in a separate repository if you expect frequent changes; this prevents your core code history from becoming cluttered.
Use semantic versioning (SemVer) for your pricing schemas, not just your code, to track when business logic evolves.
Implement a 'dry-run' mode in your pricing engine that logs calculations to the console without hitting the payment gateway.
Never hardcode discount percentages; use environment variables that can be updated via your CI/CD pipeline without a full build.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests specifically for edge cases in your billing logic, such as mid-cycle upgrades or downgrades.
resources: Git Best Practices for SaaS: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: After completing this, integrate a mock payment gateway using a sandbox environment to test the full lifecycle of a user's subscription, from sign-up to cancellation.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to build SaaS products that scale without the fear of breaking production. Stop worrying about versioning and start focusing on your user's growth. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
